title: Theoria Knowledge Card (Markdown with YAML Frontmatter)
description: Knowledge cards are stored as markdown files with YAML frontmatter at ~/.theoria/knowledge/cards/<id>.md. The frontmatter contains structured metadata (grep-searchable), and the markdown body contains human-readable findings, limitations, gotchas, and connections.

frontmatter.description: YAML frontmatter between --- delimiters. All fields are grep-searchable via: grep -l 'field: value' ~/.theoria/knowledge/cards/*.md

session_id.description: Session slug this card was generated from

taste_alignment.description: How well the research aligned with researcher taste (0.0-1.0)

memory.description: Neuroscience-grounded memory fields. Hebbian strengthening on access, Ebbinghaus decay over time.

strength.description: Hebbian strength — increases when accessed (new = min(1.0, old + 0.1)), decays over time via Ebbinghaus (decayed = strength * (1 - decay_rate) ^ days_since_access)

access_count.description: How many times this card has been referenced by orient stages

last_accessed.description: Last time orient referenced this card

created_strength.description: Initial strength assigned by curator based on evidence quality. ACCEPT = 0.7, ACCEPT_WITH_CAVEAT = 0.4

decay_rate.description: Ebbinghaus decay rate per day. Default 0.02 — strength halves in ~35 days without access. Higher = faster forgetting.

tags.description: Searchable topic tags (grep -l 'tags:' then scan the YAML list)

body.description: Markdown body after the frontmatter. Human-readable, structured with headings.

sections.title_heading: # <Card Title> — top-level heading summarizing the research
sections.key_findings: ## Key Findings — bullet list of primary results with metrics
sections.limitations: ## Limitations — known caveats, noisy metrics, small sample sizes
sections.implementation_gotchas: ## Implementation Gotchas — practical issues discovered during execution
sections.connections: ## Connections — how this relates to other work (builds on, contradicts, related to)

memory_model.description: Neuroscience-grounded memory model for knowledge card lifecycle

hebbian_strengthening.description: When the orient agent references a card during search, its strength increases

ebbinghaus_decay.description: Each day since last_accessed, strength decreases exponentially
ebbinghaus_decay.half_life_days: ~35 days at default rate (strength halves without access)
ebbinghaus_decay.note: Decay is computed on read, not written back continuously. The stored strength reflects the value at last_accessed time.

initial_strength.description: Set by the curator agent based on evidence quality of the card

pruning_thresholds.description: Cards below these thresholds are surfaced by the prune skill for review

example:
---
id: theoria-2026-04-03-sparse-ring-attention
session_id: sparse-ring-attention
domain: efficient-transformers
research_type: computational
status: completed
confidence: 0.85
taste_alignment: 0.87
memory:
  strength: 0.7
  access_count: 3
  last_accessed: 2026-04-10T14:30:00Z
  created_strength: 0.7
  decay_rate: 0.02
created_at: 2026-04-03T09:00:00Z
tags:
  - attention-mechanisms
  - long-context
  - sparse-computation
  - ring-attention
methods:
  - ablation-study
  - benchmark-comparison
papers_referenced:
  - "arxiv:2401.12345"
  - "arxiv:2502.67890"
---

# Block-Sparse Ring Attention for Long Context

## Key Findings
- Block-sparse ring attention achieves 0.92x quality at 3.1x throughput
- H200 scales 40% better than H100 due to memory bandwidth

## Limitations
- Quality metric noisy beyond 500K tokens (only 3 data points)

## Implementation Gotchas
- Ring communication overhead dominates for small block sizes (<128)
- Need custom CUDA kernel for block-sparse pattern — PyTorch native is 2x slower

## Connections
- Builds on: Ring Attention (2024), Flash Attention 3 (2025)
- Contradicts: [none]
- Related to: Linear attention approaches (different trade-off curve)
